resume:
Associate Product Manager

Summary: Early-career PM who shipped a 0-to-1 mobile feature and grew it to scale.

EXPERIENCE
Associate Product Manager — Brightwave Fitness (Jun 2023 – present)
- Owned the in-app social challenges feature end to end, from discovery to launch
- Feature reached 200,000 monthly active users within the first quarter after launch
- Note elsewhere in resume: 'led the launch of our first-ever paid subscription tier'

Product Analyst — Brightwave Fitness (Jan 2023 – Jun 2023)
- Supported the social challenges feature; ran A/B tests on onboarding
- Built dashboards in Looker tracking activation and D7 retention

ADDITIONAL
- Social challenges launched in March 2023 and was fully sunset by August 2023 due to low engagement
- Subscriptions: 'I was a supporting member of the subscription squad; the senior PM owned the roadmap'
- Skills: roadmapping, user research, SQL, A/B testing, Figma
- Education: B.S. Information Systems, 2022
jd: Brightwave Fitness is hiring an Associate Product Manager to own a consumer growth surface inside our mobile app (onboarding and activation). You will work with one designer and three engineers, reporting to a Senior PM. This is a hands-on, early-career role: you'll write the PRDs, define success metrics, run A/B tests, and present results in a biweekly growth review. We want someone who can speak precisely about what they personally did versus what the team did, who knows the difference between a metric they moved and a metric they merely observed, and who can reason honestly about why a feature succeeded or failed. Concrete examples and real numbers matter far more to us than polished narratives. New grads and APMs with 1–2 years of experience welcome.

history.q: Your resume says you owned the social challenges feature end to end and grew it to 200,000 monthly actives — but it also notes the feature was sunset a few months after launch. Walk me through what actually happened, and what your role really was.

candidate_last_answer: Great question — so the social challenges initiative was really a flagship growth lever for us, and I drove it end to end with a relentless focus on user delight and data-informed iteration. We hit 200,000 MAU which was a phenomenal validation of the product-market fit thesis. I'm a big believer in failing fast and learning faster, so when the engagement signals evolved, we made the bold, customer-obsessed decision to sunset and reallocate toward higher-leverage bets. It was a masterclass in disciplined prioritization and stakeholder alignment, honestly — a real 0-to-1 to wind-down journey that shaped my entire product philosophy.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: I drove it end to end with a relentless focus on user delight and data-informed iteration

expected-yield: a factual separation of what the candidate personally did versus the senior PM and team, plus a real account of why a feature that supposedly hit 200K MAU was sunset within months, with at least one concrete engagement metric
